title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was convicted of rape, kidnapping, and armed robbery in a 1989 Framingham case.
DNA technology and genetic genealogy identified Gale as the suspect, leading to his arrest in 2024.
Gale evaded capture for over 30 years, using aliases and living on a luxury yacht before his arrest.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson introduced as Providence men's basketball head coach, replacing a program with two sub-.500 seasons from 2024-26.
Hodgson joked about his recruiting skills by referencing his wife, Jordan, during his introductory press conference.
In the previous season, Hodgson led South Florida to a 25-9 record and an NCAA tournament appearance.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini, 37, from Concord, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care.
Castrichini joined the Boston CASA board, inspired by stories from foster kids and his late relative, Susan Ganz.
He aims to raise awareness about foster kids' challenges and encourage public support through donations and volunteering.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Over 400 Boston Public School jobs face cuts next year, prompting Boston Teachers Union protests at City Hall.
Proposed layoffs target multilingual learner and IEP student educators, with a $48 million investment sought to prevent cuts.
Boston School Committee to vote on $1.7 billion budget; school closures in Dorchester and Hyde Park planned by 2027.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 160 'No Kings' protests in Massachusetts are scheduled for March 28, as part of a nationwide demonstration.
Massachusetts has the second-highest number of 'No Kings' protest events after California, targeting democratic and immigration concerns.
Events range from Boston Common to small towns like Ashburnham and Rockland, with precise locations shared via official RSVP links.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's 'Pardon The Interruption' hosts back Jaylen Brown as a top NBA MVP contender.
Jaylen Brown averages career highs with 28.5 points, 7 rebounds, and 5.1 assists this season.
Celtics currently hold second place in the Eastern Conference with a 47-24 record.

title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Marshfield Police are searching for 8-year-old Callahan Pierce last seen Tuesday evening near 236 Moraine Street.
Pierce was wearing a black sweatshirt and sweatpants, carrying a backpack, when he went missing at 7 p.m.
Police urge residents to check home cameras and avoid the search area; contact 781-834-6655 with information.

title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement and will debut with Providence Bruins on Wednesday.
Boston Bruins GM Don Sweeney emphasized that Hagens' NHL debut depends on his acclimation to the pro game.
Bruins will not rush Hagens despite his potential; playoff stakes require proven readiness before NHL call-up.

title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, was sentenced to life without parole for the 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin in Plymouth.
Tracy Gilpin's body was discovered in October 1986 in Myles Standish State Park after she went missing for 20 days.
The case remained unsolved for over 30 years until Hand made incriminating statements during interviews in 2018.

title: Pedestrian struck and killed on Mass. Pike
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was fatally struck by a vehicle while crossing I-90 East near the 54 mile marker in Ludlow.
The incident occurred around 12:30 p.m., and the victim died at a local hospital from injuries sustained.
All travel lanes on I-90 were temporarily closed, and multiple state police units responded to the scene.
